# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: my-application
  annotations:
    linkerd.io/inject: enabled # This enables automatic proxy injection for pods in this namespace

---
# Define a ServiceAccount for your backend service.
# This ServiceAccount's identity will be used for mTLS authentication.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: backend-sa
  namespace: my-application

---
# Define a ServiceAccount for your frontend service.
# This ServiceAccount's identity will be authorized to call the backend.
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: frontend-sa
  namespace: my-application

---
# ServerAuthentication specifies the type of authentication required for incoming connections.
# Here, we define that the 'backend-server' requires authenticated mTLS connections from the mesh.
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: ServerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: backend-auth-mtls
  namespace: my-application
spec:
  client:
    meshTLS:
      # Set to 'false' to require authentication.
      # If 'true', unauthenticated connections would be allowed by this policy,
      # but an AuthorizationPolicy would still need to permit them.
      unauthenticated: false
      # Optional: You can list specific identities here, but it's more flexible
      # to define allowed identities in an AuthorizationPolicy.
      # identities:
      #   - "frontend-sa.my-application.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"

---
# Server defines how a service accepts incoming connections on a specific port.
# It selects pods with a given label and specifies the port.
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: backend-server
  namespace: my-application
spec:
  # Selects pods that have the label 'app: backend'.
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: backend
  # The port on the pod that this Server policy applies to.
  port: 8080
  # The protocol used by the application on this port.
  # Common values: HTTP/1, HTTP/2, GRPC, TCP.
  proxyProtocol: HTTP/1

---
# AuthorizationPolicy defines which clients are allowed to connect to a Server.
# This policy allows the 'frontend-sa' service account to connect to 'backend-server'.
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: allow-frontend-to-backend
  namespace: my-application
spec:
  # 'targetRef' links this AuthorizationPolicy to a specific Server resource.
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: backend-server
  # 'requiredAuthenticationRef' links this AuthorizationPolicy to a ServerAuthentication resource.
  # This ensures that clients must satisfy the authentication criteria defined in 'backend-auth-mtls'.
  requiredAuthenticationRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: ServerAuthentication
    name: backend-auth-mtls
  # Defines the clients that are permitted to connect.
  client:
    meshTLS:
      # List the mTLS identities (service accounts) that are allowed to connect.
      # The format is "[service-account-name].[namespace].serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
      identities:
        - "frontend-sa.my-application.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
        # Optional: If the backend service needs to call itself (e.g., internal health checks),
        # you might include its own identity here.
        - "backend-sa.my-application.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
      # Optional: To allow any authenticated client from the mesh:
      # anyAuthenticated: true
